title: After the sun rise [WOW] (Remix)
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
After the sunrise
You're looking at who is there
You wonder if I know
Or even if I care
Do you look at him in the eye
When he gives you love until the end
Does it feel like it's the first time
As it has so again and again and again

I see you laughing in his arms
His hands tracing what was mine
The warmth I used to feel now gone
While he takes all of  your time

[4 Beat Pause Before Chorus 1]


[Chorus 1]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold


[4 Count Pause Before Verse 2]


[Verse 2]
With power that can’t be saved
By changing the hands you hold
Changing the sheets that cover the ghost
Of new stories now so old
Empty frames collecting dust
Drawers with little left
Your footsteps echo down the hall
A reminder of your theft
You gave your heart where I can’t go
And left me drowning in the ache
Every smile he gives you now
Feels like another piece you take

[4 Beat Instrumental Pause Before Chorus 2]

[Chorus 2]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold

[Outro]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
tags: acoustic , pain, chilling, regret, anger, bitter, betrayal, mad, dark mood, confrontational, frustrated, lost, regret, rough voice, hoarse voice, punk, emo

title: After the sun rise [WOW]
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
After the sun rise
You're looking at who is there
You wonder if I know
Or even if I care
Do you look at him in the eye
When he gives you love until the end
Does it feel like it's the first time
As it has so again and again and again

Shadows stretch across the floor,
Where laughter used to live.
I keep the pieces in my hands,
Though there’s nothing more to give.

[4 Beat Pause Before Chorus 1]

[Chorus 1]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold

[Verse 2]
With power that can’t be saved
By changing the hands you hold,
Changing the sheets that cover the ghost
Of new stories now so old
Empty frames collecting dust
Drawers with little left
Scraps of paper, signatures
Evidence of what was there
[Pause]
Fading echoes in the hall,
Footsteps never find their sound.
Even love can sometimes fall,
And leave the fuckin' heart unbound.

[4 Beat Instrumental Pause Before Chorus 2]

[Chorus 2]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold

[Outro]
I have to wonder what is real
Stolen, broken, or sold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold
After the sunrise leaves half my bed so cold

title: Almost (Not Quite) [Lyrical]
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I don't know why I still love you, it's embarrassing,
deep in my chest, still craving you, it's demanding.
I walk through my city with a smile that's practiced,
good in daylight, wrecked when the day passes.
Got a girl who likes my "life," likes the shine, likes the look,
likes the posts and the motion, not the price that it took.
She thinks we good, but my smile is a cover,
she don't know my mind stays fixed on another.
I say I'm moving on, then I circle the block,
running old scenes like I forgot how to stop.
Hate that the truth is I still want you too,
so I pray you don't call me drunk at two.
'Cause if you called right now, I'd answer like a fool,
act tough for a second, then I'm back in your pull.
Never had a steady love, never had a mom to lean on,
so I chased "don't leave" 'cause I feared a "Dear John."
You saw every soft spot, knew where I would bend,
that's why it was easy making our love pretend.
Is that why you disappeared, is that why you'd betray?
'Cause you knew you were the one thing I wouldn't throw away.

[Pre-Chorus]
You got me missing you and hating you, I'm impressed,
I try to move forward, my heart starts to protest.
I deleted our thread, now I stare at the screen,
mind wants peace, but my heart wants to intervene.

[Chorus]
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
I swore you were the one, from the jump it felt right.
I keep your pictures on my phone, replay your last words to me,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said you'd ride or die, then you let our whole life burn,
left me holding the ashes like it was my turn to learn.
Where you at now, out healing or out having fun?
I'm trying to say goodbye, but I believed you were the one.

[Verse 2]
Now you tell everybody I'm the villain in your story,
like I woke up one day and chose to mine pain from your quarry.
If you want the truth, I won't hide behind the mask,
I don't gotta fake a thing, I can answer what you ask.
I was pride and pressure, I was "prove it" and "be mine,"
I made your freedom look suspicious, like your love was a crime.
I didn't buy you peace, I bought control with little things,
kept you close and called it loyalty, clipped off your wings.
I called it protection, but it was fear in a suit,
I needed you weaker so I could feel bulletproof.

You'd cry in the spotlight, make my guilt do flips,
then wake up and rewrite it like I made it all up.
You'd say "forever," but always one foot out the door,
I stayed stuck in the fairytale, ignoring what I saw.
And still... you gave me years I can't stop replaying,
little moments I hold tight when I'm sober and I'm shaking.
I don't want revenge, I just want quiet in my head,
but my thoughts run on repeat for the things I never said.
You gave me warmth when I was cold and hard to reach,
then you left and I learned silence is its own kind of speech.

[Pre-Chorus 2]
And I hate what you did, and I hate what I became,
every time I compare her to you, I start to go insane.
I laugh with someone new but always hear you instead,
like I moved on outside but my mind stayed in our bed.

[Chorus 2]
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
I swore you were the one, but you claim it ain't right.
I keep your pictures on my phone, replay your last words to me,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said you'd ride or die, then you let our whole life burn,
left me holding the ashes like it was my turn to learn.
Where you at now, out healing or out having fun?
I'm trying to say goodbye, but I believed you were the one.

[Bridge]
How I'm supposed to love you when I don't love who I am?
How I'm supposed to give you all of me when I'm split in half?
I'm a burning boat acting steady on the wave,
so don't grab on when I'm sinking, I'll pull you to the grave.
We had good days, I won't pretend they didn't happen,
but I never saw it coming: you'd be my last assassin.
Maybe we were meant to meet, just not meant to stay,
some people teach you what you want by taking it away.

[Final Chorus]
Love ain't a rescue, trust ain't a home,
it's a pretty little word when you're not alone.
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
I tried to make it work, I tried to make it right.
I keep your pictures on my phone, replay your last words to me,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said you'd ride or die, then you let our whole life burn,
left me holding the ashes like it was my turn to learn.
Where you at now, out healing or out having fun?
I'm trying to move on, but I still hope we ain't done.
tags: Lyrical rap, 96 BPM, minor key. Dark piano + tense strings, heavy 808, crisp punchy drums, fast hi-hats. Tight, clear verse delivery with dense rhyme pockets, hook more melodic but still rhythmic. Pre-chorus lifts with lighter drums, bridge drops to sparse piano then rebuilds. Final chorus hits harder with added intensity and ad-libs.

tags: Melodic emo-rap / sad trap, 92–96 BPM, minor key. Airy guitar + pads layered with dark piano, heavy 808, crisp drums. Intimate verses (half-spoken/half-rapped), bigger emotional singable hook, subtle harmonies on the chorus. Bridge stripped and vulnerable then builds into a louder final chorus with more aggression.

title: Almost (Not Quite)  [Rap  CUSTOM V2]
lyrics:
[Verse 1] 
(16 bars)
I don't know why I still love you, it's sick, it's embarrassing,
deep in my chest still craving you, the silence is deafening.
I walk through my city with a smile that's practiced,
good in daylight, wrecked when the day passes.
Got a girl that likes my "life," likes the shine and the snapshot,
likes the posts and the motion, never counted what that cost.
She thinks we good, but my face is a mask when we talk,
'cause my mind stays parked on your name like a locked box.
I say I'm moving on, then I circle the same four corners,
replaying old scenes like my mind is a TiVo recorder.
Hate that I still want you close but I've been acting phony,
so I pray you don't call me at two when you're drunk and you're lonely.
'Cause if you call right now, I'm weak, I pick up too quick,
act tough for a second, then I fold and I slip.
Never had steady love, never had nobody to lean on,
so I stayed in that chase scene like a Tom and Jerry rerun.

[Pre-Chorus]
You got me missing you and hating you, it's deadly,
I step forward, my heart yanks back, the world gets heavy.
Deleted our thread, now I'm stuck in the hurt,
my mind gets peace when this love's six feet in the dirt.

[Chorus] 
(short rap hook)
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
swore you were the one, yeah, that first night felt right.
Pictures on my phone got my head on repeat,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said "ride or die," then you dipped at the worst,
left me with the smoke and the lesson in the dirt.
Where you at now, healing up or having fun?
I'm tryna say goodbye, but I believed you were the one.

[Verse 2] 
(16 bars)
Now you tell everybody I'm the villain in your version,
like I woke up and chose to be cruel on purpose.
If you want the truth, I won't duck it, won't dodge it,
I don't gotta fake a thing, I'll say it and own it.
I was pride and pressure, "prove it," "be mine,"
turned your freedom suspicious like loving me was a crime.
Didn't buy you peace, I bought control in small signs,
kept you close, called it loyalty, clipped your ability to fly.
Called it protection, but it was fear in a suit,
I needed you weaker just to feel bulletproof.
You'd cry in the spotlight, make my guilt do loops,
then wake up and rewrite it, leave my memories in dispute.
You'd say "forever" with one foot out the door,
I stayed in the fairytale, ignored what I saw.
And still... I replay it like I got no off switch,
I don't want revenge, I want quiet, I want my life back.

[Pre-Chorus 2]
And I hate what you did, and I hate what I became,
every time I compare her to you, I go insane.
I laugh with someone new but always hear you instead,
like I moved on outside but my mind stayed in our bed.

[Chorus 2] 
(short rap hook)
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
swore you were the one, yeah, that first night felt right.
Pictures on my phone got my head on repeat,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said "ride or die," then you dipped at the worst,
left me with the smoke and the lesson in the dirt.
Where you at now, healing up or having fun?
I'm tryna say goodbye, but I believed you were the one.

[Bridge] 
(emotional release)
How I'm supposed to love you when I don't love who I am?
How I'm supposed to give you all of me when I'm not your man?
I called it "protecting us," but it was panic in disguise,
I was dodging all your questions, looking you dead in the eyes.
And you played your part too, that back-and-forth, that switch,
make me feel guilty for reacting, then say I'm the one that slipped.
We had good days, I won't pretend they didn't happen,
but the same hands that held me were the ones that did the damage.
Maybe we were meant to meet, just not meant to stay,
some people teach you what you want by taking it away.
Now I'm trying to be better, but the guilt keeps score,
every "lesson learned" feels like I'm kicking my own door.

[Final Pre-Chorus]
Love ain't a rescue, trust ain't a home,
it's a pretty little word when you're not alone.

[Final Chorus] 
(harder delivery)
You were my "right there," I was your "not quite,"
I tried to make it work, I tried to make it right.
Pictures on my phone got my head on repeat,
hover over "trash," can't survive if I hit "delete."
You said "ride or die," then you dipped at the worst,
left me with the smoke and the lesson in the dirt.
Where you at now, healing up or having fun?
I'm tryna move on, but I still hope we ain't done.

[Tag] 
(short, rap)
Right there, not quite, stuck in the same frame,
I cut you off, then I check for your name.
New face beside me, but my head in the past,
I'm learning closure ain't a button you can tap.

[Outro] 
(spoken)
Same streets, same smoke, new future, old ghosts.
Phone face-down, still loud in my head.
I'm not asking for you back.
I'm asking for the end
tags:
Modern white intelligent rap. 
Verses are aggressive 16-bar rap with clear diction (no singing in verses). 
Chorus is a short chantable rap hook with minimal melody. Bridge strips to piano then builds. 
Final chorus hits harder with ad-libs. 
Short rap tag before the outro.
